summary: Healed drifting Google Doc URLs on the tab-grouping sync path: a doc whose in-page ?tab= query has drifted since it was recorded now keeps its position in its group instead of dropping to the bottom. The drift-heal logic — previously only in the onUpdated navigation handler — was extracted into a shared helper and applied on the grouping-sync record path too.

changes.title: Heal drifted doc URLs on the grouping-sync record path
changes.description: recordInGroupTab now heals a drifted same-page slot in place before appending. When MV3 tears down the service worker and wipes in-memory state, the next group sync used to see a Google Doc whose live ?tab= query had drifted from its recorded urlKey as a non-member and append the live key to the end of the label — dropping the doc to the bottom of the group. It now locates the recorded slot for the same page (by samePageKey) and rewrites it in place, preserving the doc's position; only a genuinely-new URL still appends. The exact-key urlKeyIsMember membership test is untouched.

waysToTest.text: Read the recordInGroupTab tests in service_worker.test.js — the reproduction test asserts a drifted doc key rewrites in place at its original index rather than appending, and a sibling test confirms a genuinely-new URL still appends.

tests.name: service_worker.js › recordInGroupTab › rewrites a drifted Google Doc slot in place rather than pushing to the end
tests.description: Reproduction: a drifted Google Doc (same page, new ?tab= query) is healed at its original slot instead of being appended to the bottom of the group. Red if the drift-heal is reverted to a plain push.

tests.name: service_worker.js › recordInGroupTab › still appends a genuinely new URL that shares no page with any slot
tests.description: Guards that the drift-heal does not swallow real new members — a URL sharing no page with any recorded slot still appends.

changes.title: Extract the drift-heal into a shared helper
changes.description: The locate-by-page-identity then rewrite-in-place-or-splice-if-duplicate logic was duplicated between the onUpdated navigation handler and the new recordInGroupTab record path. Extracted it into a single healDriftedLabelSlot(label, newUrlKey, liveUrl) helper alongside samePageKey. It mutates label.urlKeys in place and returns { found, mutated, previousKey } so each caller decides whether to append (record path) and whether to persist/log (onUpdated). onUpdated was rewired to call it with identical behavior.

waysToTest.text: Read src/lib/utils/healDriftedLabelSlot.test.js — six unit tests cover in-place rewrite, splice-on-duplicate, exact-match no-op, genuinely-new not-found, empty label, and fragment-only equality.

tests.name: healDriftedLabelSlot › rewrites a drifted same-page slot in place, preserving its index
tests.description: A drifted same-page key is rewritten at its existing index, keeping position and returning previousKey.

tests.name: healDriftedLabelSlot › splices out the stale slot when the live key already exists elsewhere
tests.description: When the live key is already recorded elsewhere, the stale slot is spliced out instead of duplicating it.

tests.name: healDriftedLabelSlot › reports found without mutating when the slot already matches exactly
tests.description: An exact match reports found (so no append) but leaves the array untouched.

tests.name: healDriftedLabelSlot › reports not-found for a genuinely new page and does not mutate
tests.description: A page with no same-page slot returns not-found so the caller appends; nothing is mutated.

tests.name: healDriftedLabelSlot › is a not-found no-op on an empty label
tests.description: An empty label has no slot to heal — a not-found no-op.

tests.name: healDriftedLabelSlot › treats a fragment-only change on the same page as an exact match
tests.description: Because getUrlKey strips fragments, a fragment-only change on the same page/query is an exact match and is left untouched.
